items.title: Trump Wants Health Insurers to ‘Ease Up and Start Cutting Prices.’ What Does That Mean for UNH Stock in 2026?
items.description: Trump’s call for health insurers to cut premiums adds political pressure to an already challenged sector, raising fresh questions about UnitedHealth’s pricing power, margins, and stock outlook heading into 2026.

items.title: Earnings Preview: What To Expect From UnitedHealth's Report
items.description: UnitedHealth Group is poised to announce its fourth-quarter results next month, and analysts predict a double-digit decline in the company’s bottom-line figure.

items.title: Amentum Stock Draws Fresh Buying Amid $14.4 Billion Revenue Year and 51% Stock Run
items.author: Jonathan Ponciano
items.description: Hyperion Capital Advisors increased its stake in Amentum Holdings by 368,521 shares in Q3, adding $8.84 million to the position. Amentum, a government services contractor, reported $14.4 billion in revenue for fiscal 2025 with $47 billion in backlog and a 1.2x book-to-bill ratio. The stock has surged 51% over the past year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500's 15% gain.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong fiscal 2025 results with $14.4B revenue, $1.1B adjusted EBITDA, and $516M free cash flow. Robust $47B backlog with 1.2x book-to-bill provides clear visibility. Stock up 51% YoY, institutional investor confidence evidenced by Hyperion's increased position. Declining net leverage to 3.2x and guidance for further FCF growth in 2026 support positive outlook.

Mentioned as a top holding in Hyperion Capital's portfolio (19.2% of AUM), used as a reference point for quality, durable cash-generating businesses. No specific news or performance commentary provided in the article.

items.title: Why a 63% Vertex Selloff Didn’t Scare Off a New $13 Million Investor
items.author: Jonathan Ponciano
items.description: Hyperion Capital Advisors acquired 540,000 shares of Vertex (VERX) worth $13.39 million in Q3, making it the fund's third-largest holding. Despite a 63% stock decline over the past year, the investment signals confidence in Vertex's underlying business fundamentals, including 12.7% revenue growth, 30% cloud revenue growth, and strong cash generation with a $150 million share buyback authorization.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite severe stock price decline (63% down), the company demonstrates solid fundamentals with double-digit revenue growth (12.7%), strong cloud revenue growth (30%), positive adjusted EBITDA margins (22.6%), and $313M in cash. Institutional investor confidence and management's $150M buyback authorization suggest undervaluation and recovery potential.
Mentioned as a top holding in Hyperion's portfolio (19.2% of AUM), indicating it is a core stable position. No specific news or analysis provided in the article.
